title: ipv6_addr.h struct in6_addr detection cascade is complex but necessary
description:
ipv6_addr.h lines 41-65 have a multi-branch preprocessor cascade to detect if struct in6_addr is already defined:

```c
#if defined(LICHEN_HAVE_IN6_ADDR) && LICHEN_HAVE_IN6_ADDR
/* User says in6_addr is already defined - trust them */
#elif defined(IN6ADDR_ANY_INIT)
/* System header already provides struct in6_addr */
#elif defined(CONFIG_NET_IPV6) && defined(__ZEPHYR__)
#include <zephyr/net/net_ip.h>
#elif defined(_NETINET_IN_H) || defined(_NETINET_IN_H_) || defined(_NETINET6_IN6_H) || \
      defined(__NETINET_IN_H__) || defined(ZEPHYR_INCLUDE_POSIX_NETINET_IN_H_)
/* System header already provides struct in6_addr */
#else
struct in6_addr { uint8_t s6_addr[16]; };
#endif
```

This is correct and necessary for portability across Zephyr, POSIX, and bare-metal. The inline comments explain each branch.

What goes wrong: A reader unfamiliar with C portability patterns may not understand why this is needed. The header comment (lines 32-40) explains the strategy but is easy to miss.

Improvement: Add a block comment before the cascade explaining the problem being solved: 'struct in6_addr may be defined by system headers, Zephyr, or not at all. We detect which case applies to avoid redefinition errors.'
